title: Minimum Operations to Halve Array Sum
description:
You are given an array nums of positive integers. In one operation, you can choose any number from nums and reduce it to exactly half the number. (Note that you may choose this reduced number in future operations.)

Return _the minimum number of operations to reduce the sum of_ nums _by at least half._

solutionCode.python:
```python
import heapq

def min_operations(nums):
    max_heap = [-num for num in nums]
    heapq.heapify(max_heap)
    initial_sum = sum(nums)
    target_sum = initial_sum // 2
    sum_so_far = 0
    operations = 0

    while sum_so_far <= target_sum:
        largest = -heapq.heappop(max_heap)
        sum_so_far += largest // 2
        operations += 1

    return operations
```

    
    The algorithm starts by adding all elements from `nums` to a max_heap. We then compute the initial sum and target sum of the array values.

We start a loop where we pop the largest value from the max_heap, update the current sum to try to reach the target sum, and increment the operations counter. We continue this process until the current sum surpasses the target sum.

The time complexity of this algorithm is O(nlogn) due to the use of a priority queue (max heap).
